metadata.description: Challenges validating system responsiveness under stress conditions including concurrent parsing latency, FormatRegistry initialization under contention, cache overflow handling, rate limiter saturation recovery, non-blocking operation guarantees, connection pool exhaustion behavior, and coroutine scope stress across all protocol services and format parsers

challenges.name: Concurrent Parsing Latency Under Load
challenges.description: Verify that parsing latency for all 17 formats remains within acceptable bounds when 50 concurrent parse requests are active simultaneously. Measure p50, p95, and p99 latency for each format. P95 latency must not exceed 5x the single-threaded baseline. No parse request may time out or fail due to resource contention.

outputs.name: latency_results
outputs.description: Map of format ID to p50/p95/p99 latency under concurrency

outputs.name: exceeded_threshold
outputs.description: Formats whose p95 latency exceeded 5x baseline

outputs.name: failed_requests
outputs.description: Parse requests that timed out or failed under load

assertions.message: No format may have p95 latency exceeding 5x its single-threaded baseline
No parse request may fail or time out under concurrent load
All latency measurements must produce valid numeric values

challenges.name: Concurrent Parsing Fairness Across Formats
challenges.description: Verify that no single format parser starves other parsers under concurrent load. Submit equal numbers of parse requests for all 17 formats simultaneously and verify that completion times are within 3x of each other. A heavyweight format (LaTeX) should not block lightweight formats (plaintext) from completing promptly.

outputs.name: completion_times
outputs.description: Map of format ID to average completion time

outputs.name: starved_formats
outputs.description: Formats whose completion time was disproportionately delayed

outputs.name: fairness_ratio_met
outputs.description: Whether the fairness ratio was met across all formats

assertions.message: No format parser may be starved by other concurrent parsers
Completion time fairness ratio must be within 3x across all formats
All completion time measurements must be valid

challenges.name: FormatRegistry Initialization Under Contention
challenges.description: Verify that FormatRegistry lazy initialization is thread-safe when 100 concurrent coroutines access the formats list simultaneously during first initialization. Only one initialization must occur (no duplicate work). All coroutines must receive the same fully-initialized format list. The lazy delegate must not deadlock or throw ConcurrentModificationException.

outputs.name: init_results
outputs.description: Results of concurrent initialization attempts

outputs.name: initialization_count
outputs.description: Number of times createFormats() was actually invoked

outputs.name: all_received_same_list
outputs.description: Whether all coroutines received the identical format list

outputs.name: exceptions_thrown
outputs.description: Any exceptions thrown during concurrent initialization

assertions.message: createFormats() must be invoked exactly once regardless of concurrent access
All coroutines must receive the same fully-initialized format list
No exceptions may be thrown during concurrent lazy initialization

challenges.name: DocumentCache Overflow Under Rapid Insertion
challenges.description: Verify that DocumentCache handles rapid insertion beyond its maximum capacity without data corruption, deadlocks, or memory leaks. Insert 10x the cache capacity in rapid succession from 20 concurrent coroutines. The cache size must never exceed the configured maximum. All cache operations must return valid results without throwing exceptions.

outputs.name: overflow_results
outputs.description: Cache state snapshots during overflow testing

outputs.name: max_size_observed
outputs.description: Maximum cache size observed during the test

outputs.name: corruption_detected
outputs.description: Cache entries that returned corrupted or incorrect data

outputs.name: exceptions_thrown
outputs.description: Exceptions thrown during cache operations

assertions.message: No cache entry may return corrupted or incorrect data during overflow
No exceptions may be thrown during cache overflow handling
All cache operations must return valid results during overflow

challenges.name: DocumentCache Concurrent Read-Write Consistency
challenges.description: Verify that DocumentCache maintains read-write consistency when simultaneous reads and writes target the same cache keys. Launch 10 writer coroutines and 40 reader coroutines operating on overlapping key sets. Readers must never observe partially written entries or stale data after a write completes. No ConcurrentModificationException may occur.

outputs.name: consistency_results
outputs.description: Read-write consistency verification results

outputs.name: partial_reads
outputs.description: Reads that returned partially written data

outputs.name: stale_reads
outputs.description: Reads that returned stale data after confirmed write

assertions.message: No read may return partially written cache data
No read may return stale data after a write to the same key completes
All consistency checks must pass

challenges.name: RateLimiter Saturation and Recovery
challenges.description: Verify that RateLimiter recovers gracefully after saturation. Saturate the TokenBucket by sending requests at 10x the configured rate for 5 seconds, then reduce to normal rate and verify recovery within 2 seconds. During saturation, excess requests must be throttled (not dropped or errored). After recovery, throughput must return to the configured rate within tolerance.

outputs.name: saturation_results
outputs.description: Request throughput during saturation and recovery phases

outputs.name: recovery_time_ms
outputs.description: Time to recover to normal throughput after saturation

outputs.name: dropped_requests
outputs.description: Requests that were dropped instead of throttled during saturation

assertions.message: No requests may be dropped during rate limiter saturation
Rate limiter must recover to normal throughput within 2 seconds
All saturation and recovery metrics must be valid

challenges.name: Non-Blocking Operation Guarantees
challenges.description: Verify that all public API methods on FormatRegistry, DocumentCache, and StyleSheets complete without blocking the calling coroutine's thread. Measure wall-clock time vs suspend time for each operation. No operation may block a thread for more than 10ms (excluding actual computation). Verify that suspending operations properly yield to the dispatcher.

outputs.name: blocking_results
outputs.description: Map of operation to wall-clock time and thread-block time

outputs.name: blocking_operations
outputs.description: Operations that blocked a thread beyond the threshold

outputs.name: operations_tested
outputs.description: Number of operations tested for non-blocking behavior

assertions.message: No operation may block a thread for more than 10ms
At least 10 critical operations must be tested for non-blocking behavior
All non-blocking measurements must produce valid results

challenges.name: Dispatcher Yield Verification Under Load
challenges.description: Verify that long-running format parsing operations properly yield to the coroutine dispatcher. Parse a large document (100KB) on a single-threaded dispatcher while also scheduling 10 other lightweight coroutines. The lightweight coroutines must make progress (not starved) during the long parse. Cooperative cancellation via yield() must be verified.

outputs.name: yield_results
outputs.description: Progress tracking for lightweight coroutines during long parse

outputs.name: starved_coroutines
outputs.description: Lightweight coroutines that made no progress during the long parse

outputs.name: cancellation_responsive
outputs.description: Whether cancellation was responsive during long parse

assertions.message: No lightweight coroutine may be starved during a long parse operation
Cooperative cancellation must be responsive during long-running parses
All yield verification results must be valid

challenges.name: Connection Pool Exhaustion Handling
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services handle connection pool exhaustion gracefully when ConnectionLimiter reaches its maximum. Exhaust all permits in the ConnectionLimiter and then issue 50 additional requests. The additional requests must queue and eventually complete (not fail with exceptions). Queue timeout must be configurable and requests exceeding the timeout must fail with a clear error message.

outputs.name: exhaustion_results
outputs.description: Results of connection pool exhaustion per protocol

outputs.name: unhandled_exceptions
outputs.description: Requests that failed with unhandled exceptions

outputs.name: queued_and_completed
outputs.description: Number of overflow requests that queued and eventually completed

outputs.name: timed_out_with_message
outputs.description: Number of requests that timed out with a clear error message

assertions.message: No request may fail with an unhandled exception during pool exhaustion
All pool exhaustion results must show either queued completion or clear timeout error

challenges.name: Rapid Connect/Disconnect Cycle Stress
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services handle 100 rapid connect/disconnect cycles without resource leaks, orphaned coroutines, or state corruption. After all cycles complete, verify that serviceScope is properly cancelled, no background coroutines are running, and the service can accept new connections normally. Memory usage must not grow unboundedly across cycles.

outputs.name: cycle_results
outputs.description: Per-protocol results of rapid reconnection cycles

outputs.name: resource_leaks
outputs.description: Resource leaks detected after all cycles completed

outputs.name: orphaned_coroutines
outputs.description: Orphaned coroutines detected after all cycles completed

outputs.name: post_cycle_functional
outputs.description: Whether services accept new connections normally after all cycles

assertions.message: No resource leaks may occur across rapid connect/disconnect cycles
No orphaned coroutines may remain after rapid reconnection cycles
Services must accept new connections normally after stress cycles

challenges.name: Large Document Parsing Under Memory Pressure
challenges.description: Verify that parsing very large documents (1MB, 5MB, 10MB) does not cause OutOfMemoryError or excessive GC pauses. Parse large documents for each of the 17 formats and measure peak memory usage and GC pause times. Peak memory during parsing must not exceed 10x the document size. The parser must not hang or become unresponsive on any large input.

outputs.name: memory_results
outputs.description: Map of format ID and size to peak memory usage and GC metrics

outputs.name: oom_errors
outputs.description: Formats that caused OutOfMemoryError at any document size

outputs.name: hung_parsers
outputs.description: Parsers that became unresponsive on large input

assertions.message: No format parser may throw OutOfMemoryError on documents up to 10MB
No parser may hang or become unresponsive on large input
All memory measurements must show peak usage within acceptable bounds
